topic: Someone receives flowers with no card attached
raw_output:
[story 1]

The box sat on my kitchen counter, a garish explosion of pink roses and wilting lilies. I stared at them, my jaw tight. Who sends flowers without a note? It was a baffling gesture, an unsolicited declaration of affection delivered with the subtlety of a foghorn. I picked up a stem, the petals feeling unnaturally smooth under my thumb, and let it drop back into the cardboard confines. I sighed, a long, drawn-out sound that seemed to vibrate the silence of my apartment. I was going to have to find the sender. This was just… inconvenient.

[story 2]

My eyes scanned the vibrant bouquet, a cascade of sunflowers and surprisingly cheerful daisies. I let out a sharp, involuntary exhale, my shoulders rising toward my ears. Another anonymous gift. I paced the length of the living room three times before stopping abruptly, running a hand through my already messy hair. Why couldn't people just put a little paper on the thing? It felt like a deliberate slight, a theatrical flourish of sentimentality without any actual substance. I kicked lightly at the base of the arrangement, a small burst of frustrated energy escaping me.

[story 3]

I picked up the arrangement, the cellophane crinkling under my grip. The scent of the flowers was overwhelming, cloying even. I turned the box over, searching frantically for any clue—a stray piece of paper, a hidden tag. Nothing. Just blooms, utterly uncontextualized. I dropped it onto the floor with a decisive thud, the sound echoing too loudly in the quiet room. I rubbed the bridge of my nose, feeling a slow, simmering irritation build behind my eyes. This was just too much effort for zero communication.
